title: Tempting Light Indian Poppy Seeds Samosas
description: A sweet and creamy Indian dish featuring poppy seeds and bonito-shave, served in a cedar-plank vessel, high in protein and perfect for a light meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender, combine the toasted poppy seeds, bonito-shave, cream cheese, plain yogurt, and honey, and blend until smooth.
Roll out the samoa pastry to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of pastry using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of the poppy seed mixture onto the center of each pastry circle.
Fold the pastry in half over the filling to form a triangle or cone shape, and press the edges together to seal.
Place the samosas onto the soaked cedar planks, leaving about 1 inch of space between each samosa.
Brush the tops of the samosas with a little bit of water and sprinkle with chopped cilantro.
Bake the samosas in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
Serve the samosas warm, garnished with additional chopped cilantro if desired.
